name: g2d3c: Mittag-Leffler / fractional-relaxation kernel -- decisive zero-GPU test
origin: convergence review #5 generator design g1d3. The judges flagged the head-to-head as ~0 GPU (re-analysis of committed curves) and would_be=escaping. Tests whether the lam(W) log-law g2d3/g2d3b found is the fingerprint of a SCALE-FREE fractional memory.
insight: Force-fitting a single exponential to a Mittag-Leffler kernel E_beta(-(lam0*dS)^beta) over a window set by cooldown width W yields an APPARENT single-pole rate lam_eff(W) ~ lam0 * W^-(1-beta) -- a POWER law in W, i.e. log lam_eff = const - (1-beta)*log W. This is EXACTLY the log-linear shape g2d3 noticed (lam ~ a - b log W) but had no mechanism for. One global fractional order beta would unify the probe(~15)-vs-sharp(~5) lam gap and make c(rho) derived.
relation_to_closed_direction: The closed 'Lomax/2-exp/stretched kernel SHAPE collapses to one-pole' direction tested heavy tails on SINGLE-CURVE residual fits (the kernels did not beat exp1 there). g2d3c tests a DIFFERENT observable the closure never had: the lam_eff(W) CONCENTRATION-LADDER signature. The two tests below resolve whether the closure extends to the ladder.

tests.T1_ladder_signature: On the full 11-point wide lam(W) ladder (g2d3 + g2d3b), fit BOTH (a) log-linear lam=A-B*log10(W) and (b) power-law log(lam)=C-(1-beta)*log10(W) [<=> lam=C'*W^-(1-beta)]. The power law is the fractional-memory signature; the log-linear must extrapolate to NEGATIVE lam at large W (unphysical). Compare on the WIDE range (W up to 5120).
tests.T2_heldout_MAE: Re-run the kernel-shape held-out comparison (explore_kernels.py P-B protocol: fit kernel+kappa on the 3 wsdcon probes, evaluate MAE change vs MPL on held-out wsd+wsdld) for exp1 vs a heavy-tailed (lomax/stretched, the tractable Mittag-Leffler proxies) kernel, with ONE global shape param. Does the heavy tail beat exp1 held-out now? (the closed direction says it collapses; T2 re-checks under the ladder-informed framing).

verdicts.FRACTIONAL_CONFIRMED: T1: power law fits the wide ladder with R2>=0.9 AND lower AIC than log-linear AND beta in (0,1); AND T2: the heavy-tailed kernel beats exp1 on held-out cross-family MAE by >=10% relative -> SHIP a fractional/scale-free memory kernel (one global beta replaces the lam-family gap); upgrade formula + paper.
verdicts.FRACTIONAL_PARTIAL: T1 supports the power law (R2>=0.9, lower AIC, beta in (0,1)) BUT T2 still collapses (heavy tail does NOT beat exp1 held-out) -> report the MECHANISTIC insight (the apparent lam(W) log-law is the fingerprint of a scale-free memory) as a measured explanation, but do NOT replace the deployed kernel (consistent with the Lomax-collapse closure: the heavy tail adds no held-out predictive gain). Honest narrowing, no formula change.
verdicts.FRACTIONAL_REJECTED: T1: power law does NOT beat log-linear on the wide ladder (similar/worse AIC, or beta outside (0,1)) -> no fractional signature; g2d3 AMBIG stands unchanged; the lam(W) trend is a mild empirical concentration effect with no scale-free-memory account.

dof_note: if CONFIRMED, ONE global beta replaces the single lam_slow (net DOF neutral) and is gated on BOTH the ladder power-law fit AND a >=10% held-out cross-family MAE gain; else rejected. G5: MPL backbone not refit verdict-bearing.
